title: The Woman King
plot: A historical epic inspired by true events that took place in The Kingdom of Dahomey, one of the most powerful states of Africa in the 18th and 19th centuries.
actors: Viola Davis, Thuso Mbedu, Lashana Lynch

categories.label: women holding formal place in arms
categories.description: Some African states make room for women within royal and military institutions, linking service, discipline, and status directly to the crown. What you'll see: training grounds, barracks life, ceremonial inspections, strict hierarchies of command, and women moving in spaces of power usually reserved for armed retainers.

categories.label: captivity and slavery structuring social order
categories.description: Unfreedom exists within the region as well as across the ocean, shaping households, labour, military service, and the fate of those taken in war. What you'll see: captives guarded after raids, enslaved labour within compounds and fields, household dependants of different rank, and constant attention to who is free and who is owned.

title: Solomon Kane
plot: Once a mercenary of Queen Elizabeth I fighting Ottomans in Africa, Solomon met the Devil's Reaper and discovered he was bound for Hell. Barely escaping, he soon renounced violence to atone for his past sins, seeking out redemption in a life of peace. That is until the followers of the sorcerer Malachi kidnap a Puritan girl, Meredith Crowthorn, and brutally slaughter her family before his eyes, forcing Solomon to take up arms and return to his violent ways once more to rescue her.
actors: James Purefoy, Max von Sydow, Rachel Hurd-Wood

categories.label: firearms changing the balance of war
categories.description: Gunpowder weapons do not erase older fighting methods, but they alter tactics, prestige, and the terms on which states bargain with neighbours and foreign merchants. What you'll see: muskets alongside blades and spears, drilling and powder stores, volleys in battle, and the visible value placed on weapons from overseas.
